data.title: Shenzhen Investment Holdings Bay Area Development posts provisional March toll revenue figures
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest update is out from Shenzhen Investment Holdings Bay Area Development Company ( (HK:0737) ).

Shenzhen Investment Holdings Bay Area Development Company reported unaudited toll revenue for March 2026 from its three main expressways, with the GS Superhighway generating RMB 240.9 million, GZ West Superhighway RMB 105.5 million and the Coastal Expressway (Shenzhen Section) RMB 74.3 million. The disclosure provides investors with a timely snapshot of traffic and revenue performance across the group’s core road assets, but the company cautioned that these monthly figures are provisional, subject to settlement adjustments and reconciliation under the integrated toll collection system, and should be used carefully as an indicator of operating trends.

The company emphasized that March data are compiled from internal unaudited records and may differ from figures in subsequent periodic reports once confirmation and settlement procedures are completed. This warning underscores the limitations of relying on single-month statistics for investment decisions and highlights the operational complexity of toll data collection in a consolidated regional network, reinforcing the need for stakeholders to consider finalized results for a more accurate assessment of performance.

More about Shenzhen Investment Holdings Bay Area Development Company

Shenzhen Investment Holdings Bay Area Development Company Limited is a Cayman Islands–incorporated operator and investor in toll expressways in the Guangdong–Hong Kong–Macao Greater Bay Area. The group manages key routes including the GS Superhighway, GZ West Superhighway and the Coastal Expressway (Shenzhen Section), serving regional transportation and logistics demand in southern China.

Average Trading Volume: 222,630

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: HK$6.01B

data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

ZX Inc. ( (HK:9890) ) has shared an announcement.

Tanwan Inc. has reported that, under its 2026 Restricted Share Unit Scheme adopted in February 2026, a trustee purchased 100,000 company shares on the market on April 17, 2026, at an average price of about HK$15.12, for a total consideration of roughly HK$1.5 million. The shares, representing approximately 0.02% of the company’s issued share capital excluding treasury shares, are being held on trust for eligible participants, bringing the total number of shares held under the scheme to 400,000 and signaling ongoing implementation of equity-based incentives that may help align employee interests with those of shareholders and support talent retention.

The transaction underscores Tanwan Inc.’s continued use of share-based remuneration as a strategic tool to incentivize and retain key personnel within the group. By modestly increasing the pool of shares earmarked for the restricted share unit scheme, the company is reinforcing its commitment to long-term incentives, which can influence its capital structure over time and may be viewed positively by stakeholders focused on governance and alignment of management with shareholder value.

More about ZX Inc.

Tanwan Inc., incorporated in the Cayman Islands and listed in Hong Kong, operates through its subsidiaries and PRC operating entities, though the announcement does not detail its specific industry or core products. The company is governed by a board comprising executive and independent non-executive directors, reflecting a typical Hong Kong-listed corporate governance structure.

data.title: China Tower Sets 2025 AGM to Approve Results, Dividend and Share-Issuance Mandate
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

China Tower ( (HK:0788) ) just unveiled an update.

China Tower has called its 2025 annual general meeting for 15 May 2026 in Hong Kong, where shareholders will vote on the approval of the 2025 financial statements, board and supervisory committee reports, the auditor’s report, and the 2026 budget. The meeting will also decide on the 2025 profit distribution and final dividend, authorize the board to set an interim profit distribution plan for 2026, and approve the reappointment of KPMG and KPMG Huazhen LLP as international and domestic auditors.

In addition, shareholders will consider a special resolution granting the board a general mandate to issue up to 20% of the existing domestic and H-share capital, excluding treasury shares, during a defined period until the next AGM or earlier revocation. This share issuance authority, which also covers offers and options that may be exercised after the mandate period, would give China Tower flexibility to raise capital and manage its equity structure, potentially affecting future dilution and funding capacity for expansion or other corporate purposes.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:0788) stock is a Hold with a HK$11.60 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on China Tower stock, see the HK:0788 Stock Forecast page.

More about China Tower

China Tower Corporation Limited is a Chinese telecommunications infrastructure company that operates tower and related facilities across the country. Listed in Hong Kong, it focuses on providing shared wireless communication tower services to mobile network operators and other customers, supporting the build-out and operation of telecom networks.

data.description:

  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An announcement from Ridge Outdoor International Limited ( (HK:2720) ) is now available.

Ridge Outdoor International Limited has announced new electronic dissemination arrangements for its corporate communications in line with updated Hong Kong listing rules and its articles of association. From 17 April 2026, most communications to shareholders will be provided electronically, with printed copies supplied only upon request.

Actionable corporate communications will be sent individually by email, and if a valid email is not available, a printed version and a request form for an email address will be mailed. Other corporate communications will be posted on the company’s and the stock exchange’s websites, and shareholders are encouraged to monitor these directly or request free printed copies if they have difficulty accessing online materials.

The company is asking shareholders to register functional email addresses via an online form or by contacting the Hong Kong branch share registrar, helping Ridge Outdoor streamline compliance and reduce reliance on paper. The shift underscores a broader market move toward digital investor communication, with implications for how shareholders access information and exercise their rights going forward.

More about Ridge Outdoor International Limited

Ridge Outdoor International Limited is a Cayman Islands-incorporated company listed in Hong Kong under stock code 2720. The company operates in the outdoor products sector, serving investors and shareholders through public markets in Hong Kong and complying with the Stock Exchange’s listing and disclosure requirements.

The latest announcement is out from Twintek Investment Holdings Ltd. ( (HK:6182) ).

Twintek Investment Holdings Ltd. has announced significant changes to its board, with the resignation of independent non-executive directors Gu Feng and Wu Yi, who stepped down due to other business commitments but reported no disagreements with the board. At the same time, the company appointed Choi Chi Fai as an executive director and named Eric Todd and Lam Cheung Shing, Richard as new independent non-executive directors, moves that are likely to strengthen its financial and governance expertise amid a continued suspension of trading in its shares.

data.title: Rongta Technology Audit Probes Deepen, Results Delay and Trading Halt Persist
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Rongta Technology (Xiamen) Group Co. Ltd. Class H ( (HK:9881) ) has issued an update.

Rongta Technology (Xiamen) Group Co. Ltd. has disclosed further details on audit issues that have delayed publication of its 2025 annual results and led to a continued trading suspension in Hong Kong. The company said auditors are scrutinizing two sets of transactions involving a shareholder-guaranteed bank loan routed offshore and sizable prepayments for overseas advertising that were later refunded.

The first transaction centers on a RMB32.68 million bank loan to the group, guaranteed and effectively funded by its controlling shareholder and transferred via a related party into a Hong Kong fund before being fully settled. Auditors have questioned the commercial rationale, substance, approval process and regulatory compliance of this arrangement, despite the company’s assertion that it carried no risk to the group.

The second transaction involves about US$4.1 million in prepayments to an independent advertising agency to support overseas expansion, which were entirely refunded by year-end 2025. Auditors have raised similar concerns over the business rationale, internal approval procedures and adherence to internal controls and disclosure rules, leaving investors facing prolonged uncertainty over the company’s financial reporting and governance standards.

More about Rongta Technology (Xiamen) Group Co. Ltd. Class H

Rongta Technology (Xiamen) Group Co. Ltd. is a mainland China-based company listed in Hong Kong, operating through a group structure and focusing on overseas business development. The company has outlined strategies to enhance its international presence and expand market share in key global markets, supported by planned marketing and financial arrangements for its overseas operations.

data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

New Hope Service Holdings Limited ( (HK:3658) ) has issued an announcement.

New Hope Service Holdings Limited has agreed, through an indirect wholly owned subsidiary, to acquire an additional 45.5% equity interest in a non-wholly owned subsidiary for RMB74.85 million. Following completion, the group’s stake in the target company will rise to 96.5% through the purchaser and Chengdu Xingfan, significantly increasing its control over the entity.

Because the seller is a substantial shareholder in the target and thus a connected person at the subsidiary level, the deal is classified both as a discloseable and connected transaction under Hong Kong listing rules. The board, including independent non-executive directors, has endorsed the terms as fair and on normal commercial terms or better, allowing the transaction to proceed with reporting and announcement obligations but without requiring shareholder approval or an independent circular.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:3658) stock is a Buy with a HK$2.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on New Hope Service Holdings Limited stock, see the HK:3658 Stock Forecast page.

More about New Hope Service Holdings Limited

New Hope Service Holdings Limited is a Hong Kong-listed company incorporated in the Cayman Islands, operating through subsidiaries in property-related or service-oriented businesses. It manages its operations via wholly owned and non-wholly owned units, using equity acquisitions to consolidate control over key subsidiaries and align interests with major shareholders.

data.title: Long Investment to Issue New Shares for Minority Stakes in Two NASDAQ Firms
data.description:

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

China Financial Leasing Group Ltd ( (HK:2312) ) just unveiled an update.

Long Investment Corp has agreed to issue 40,601,429 new shares under its general mandate at HK$0.728 per share to acquire minority stakes of less than 5% each in two NASDAQ-listed companies, Metalpha Technology Holding Limited and NIP Group Inc., from independent sellers. The consideration shares represent about 9.75% of the company’s existing share capital and 8.89% of the enlarged share base, with the issue price set at a premium to recent market prices, though completion remains subject to conditions, meaning the transactions may not proceed and could result in shareholder dilution if they do.

More about China Financial Leasing Group Ltd

Long Investment Corp is a Hong Kong-listed investment company that engages in equity investments, including minority stakes in overseas listed firms. The company uses share issuance under its general mandate as a tool to fund acquisitions and expand its portfolio exposure to international capital markets.
